title: Check What Inputs Software Uses To Assess Tote Fullness
summary: Use the training source to identify the documented inputs software uses to determine how full a tote or location is as items accumulate. The source supports weight and dimensions as referenced inputs and notes that dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.
when_to_use: Use this reference procedure when you need to explain or verify, from the training source only, what inputs the software considers when assessing tote or location fullness as parcels accumulate.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to derive exact fullness formulas or calculation logic.
Do not use this runbook to determine exact thresholds beyond what the source explicitly states.
Do not use this runbook to invent package-type scaling rules, system fields, or unsupported controls.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the training material and state only the source-supported inputs and limitations for software-determined tote fullness assessment.

safety_notes: This is a reference and interpretation procedure based on training material, not a physical intervention.
Do not invent formulas, package-type rules, or system fields not present in the source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the training slide or transcript describing tote fullness assessment

access_required: Access to UPSFORTNA OptiSweep Training Day 1 source material
system_preconditions: Relevant training slide or transcript segment is available for review

visual_references.description: Lifecycle of a parcel slide showing software determination of tote or location fullness and references to weight and dimensions.

steps.title: Locate the parcel lifecycle fullness material
steps.purpose: Find the source-backed training content that describes how software determines tote or location fullness.
steps.instruction: Open the parcel lifecycle training material and locate the slide or segment stating that software determines how full the location or tote is as items sort into it.
steps.expected_result: The relevant lifecycle slide or segment is identified for review.
steps.healthy_condition: You can see source content describing parcel flow into a tote and stating that software determines how full the location or tote is as items accumulate.
steps.failure_condition: The relevant lifecycle content cannot be located or the available content does not show the fullness assessment description.

actions.label: Locate training segment
actions.exact_action: Locate the parcel lifecycle material describing how software determines how full a location or tote is as items sort into it.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Slide titled "Lifecycle of a parcel" showing software determination of tote or location fullness.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the referenced training slide or segment is unavailable.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR text references weight and dimensions, and says software determines how full a location or tote is as items sort; dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.

steps.title: Identify the documented fullness inputs
steps.purpose: Extract the specific source-supported inputs used by software for fullness assessment.
steps.instruction: Identify the inputs explicitly referenced by the source for fullness assessment. Record that the source references weight and dimensions as inputs used by software as items accumulate in the tote or location.
steps.expected_result: The documented inputs are captured as weight and dimensions.
steps.healthy_condition: The identified inputs match the source wording and remain limited to weight and dimensions.
steps.failure_condition: Additional unsupported inputs are assumed or the source-supported inputs are not clearly identified.

actions.label: Extract source inputs
actions.exact_action: Identify the source-provided inputs referenced for fullness assessment, including weight and dimensions.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Text on the lifecycle slide referencing weight and dimensions in software fullness determination.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if more detailed calculation logic is requested, because the source does not provide it.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR text references weight and dimensions, and says software determines how full a location or tote is as items sort; dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.
The slide explains that software determines how full the location or tote is as items accumulate and references weight and dimensions.

steps.title: Capture the package-type scaling note
steps.purpose: Preserve the source limitation and nuance that dimensions are adjusted by software based on package type.
steps.instruction: Record that the source states dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type. Do not add any package-type rules beyond that statement.
steps.expected_result: The package-type scaling note is documented exactly at the level supported by the source.
steps.healthy_condition: The note is limited to the source statement that dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.
steps.failure_condition: Specific scaling formulas, percentages, or package-type logic are added without source support.

actions.label: Record scaling note
actions.exact_action: Note that the source says dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Source text indicating that dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if exact scaling rules or package-type logic are needed, because the source does not provide them.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR text references weight and dimensions, and says software determines how full a location or tote is as items sort; dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.

steps.title: Compare the assessment to documented factors only
steps.purpose: Keep any interpretation grounded in the source-supported factors only.
steps.instruction: When discussing or reviewing tote fullness assessment, compare it only to the documented source factors: software-determined accumulation using weight and dimensions, with the source note that dimensions are scaled by package type.
steps.expected_result: The explanation or comparison remains limited to source-supported factors.
steps.healthy_condition: The comparison uses only the documented factors and does not introduce unsupported logic.
steps.failure_condition: The comparison relies on invented formulas, thresholds, fields, or assumptions not present in the source.

actions.label: Constrain comparison to source factors
actions.exact_action: Compare the observed or discussed fullness assessment to the documented inputs, using only the source-provided factors.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if exact thresholds, formulas, or system field mappings are requested.
Stop and escalate if unsupported package-type rules would be required to continue.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR text references weight and dimensions, and says software determines how full a location or tote is as items sort; dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.

steps.title: Record that fullness is software-determined
steps.purpose: Document the source-backed conclusion that fullness is determined by software as items accumulate rather than by manual estimation.
steps.instruction: Record that the source describes fullness assessment as software-determined as items sort into the tote or location and accumulate. Do not describe it as a manual estimate unless another source explicitly supports that, which this runbook does not use.
steps.expected_result: The final reference statement reflects software-determined fullness assessment using the documented inputs.
steps.healthy_condition: The final statement says software determines fullness as items accumulate and references weight and dimensions with package-type scaling of dimensions.
steps.failure_condition: The final statement implies manual estimation or adds unsupported logic.

actions.label: Document software determination
actions.exact_action: Record that fullness assessment is software-determined as items accumulate, rather than manually estimated.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Lifecycle slide statement that software determines how full the location or tote is as items sort into it.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if a user needs exact calculation logic or exact thresholds not provided by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR text references weight and dimensions, and says software determines how full a location or tote is as items sort; dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.

success_criteria: The user can state that the documented fullness assessment is software-determined as items accumulate.
The user can identify weight and dimensions as the source-supported inputs.
The user can state that dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.
No unsupported formulas, thresholds, or system fields are introduced.
healthy_conditions: The explanation remains grounded in the training source.
Weight and dimensions are identified as the documented inputs.
Software determination of fullness is clearly distinguished from manual estimation.
The package-type scaling note is preserved without added detail.
failure_conditions: Exact calculation logic or thresholds are required but not available in the source.
Unsupported formulas, package-type rules, or system fields are introduced.
The source material cannot be located or interpreted confidently from the packet evidence.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if more detailed calculation logic or exact thresholds are needed, because the source does not provide them.
Escalate for SME review if a user needs package-type scaling details beyond the source statement.
Escalate if the training evidence is insufficient to answer a site-specific or system-field-specific question.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Lifecycle of a parcel slide showing software determination of tote or location fullness, with references to weight and dimensions.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: OCR text references weight and dimensions, and says software determines how full a location or tote is as items sort; dimensions are scaled up by software based on package type.

missing_details: The source does not provide exact fullness formulas.
The source does not provide exact thresholds for fullness calculation in this candidate evidence.
The source does not provide detailed package-type scaling rules.
The source does not identify specific system fields or UI elements for viewing the calculation.
